message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this plan through ONE lens. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Check /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board and /home/polaris/wt/flywheel.

DIAGNOSIS: FOUR MEASURED CAUSES + TWO STRATEGIC ERRORS. Measured: (1) ATTRIBUTION INVISIBLE — all 240 [n] markers and the reference list are deleted by the RACE cleaner before judging (cleaner_survival_test.py: 0/12 survival), so the judge reads naked assertions on a task whose instruction is 'only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles'; (2) CROSS-SOURCE INFERENCE BANNED BY CONSTRUCTION — entailment_judge.py NEUTRAL clause ('introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN', /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py) plus synthesis_contract.py:197-205 delete exactly the sentence class that wins INSIGHT (weight 0.32, our worst dim 0.4238); (3) PHYSICALLY UNREADABLE — 12 paragraphs, median 677 words, 0 H3 (verified vs rank10_sections_compose/report.md); readability 0.3774 is worst on the board and bleeds into the single-call judging of all other dims; (4) CORPUS MIS-AIMED — 997 rows/919 URLs but only ~17-22 on-topic journal articles, 5 rows with authors, grey-lit top domains; the PG_AGENTIC loop was never pointed at this task. Strategic errors in the brief that this plan corrects: (A) UNITS — 0.5578 is cellcog's 100-task corpus-wide score; cellcog has NO task-72 raw_results; regression estimate of its task-72 is ~0.556±0.019, and the highest MEASURED task-72 score is bodhi's 0.5441. (B) INVERSION — the brief frames cellcog's un-sourced synthesis as a weakness to out-verify; it is the engineered strength behind insight 0.5708 at weight 0.32 (a dedicated 'Contested Debates and Novel Insights' section of 8 named, epistemically tagged syntheses 'no single paper articulates'). The winning play is: copy cellcog's insight engine and monograph architecture, keep our deterministic no-fabrication floor underneath it, and out-gun it on evidence volume/recency/method-texture where the rubric verbatim pays for 'Data and Factual Support' and 'Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized'.
RETRIEVAL: JOURNAL-FIRST AGENTIC LOOP + CITATION-GRAPH SNOWBALL, STOPPED BY A COVERAGE MATRIX, NOT A COUNT. (a) QUERY-GEN: run the existing planner perspectives (/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/agents/planner.py:309-531, PG_AGENTIC_SEED_QUERIES) but seed perspectives FROM the coverage matrix below, one per cell-group; queries phrased as scholarly-literature queries ('meta-analysis robot adoption employment', 'algorithmic management working conditions journal'), not news queries. (b) SEARCH: enable the idle loop — PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED=1 (/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/state.py:219), PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS=15 (state.py:220, default 12), PG_AGENTIC_MAX_QUERIES=160 (state.py:221); INVERT the channel mix: PG_AGENTIC_ACADEMIC_PER_ROUND 2→8 and PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND 6→2 (state.py:229-230) so OpenAlex/Semantic-Scholar-class results dominate rounds (loop entry searcher.py:291). (c) EXPANSION — the decisive stage keyword search provably cannot do (Autor-Levy-Murnane's title contains neither 'AI' nor 'labor market'): after every 3 rounds, feed all newly found DOIs into /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_build.py citation-graph expansion, BOTH directions (references + cited-by via Crossref/OpenAlex), depth 2 from canonical anchors (Autor-Levy-Murnane 2003; Acemoglu-Restrepo 2020; Frey-Osborne; Brynjolfsson-Mitchell-Rock; the 2024-25 meta-analyses). (d) FETCH: journal_corpus_fetch.py + deep_fetch.py (Crossref abstracts + Unpaywall OA full text); require retrievable abstract-or-fulltext, else keep metadata-only for context citations. (e) SELECT: hard gate = has DOI + journal + AUTHOR NAMES (the old corpus had 5/997 with authors — automatic reject now) + English; LLM on-topic gate scoring title+abstract against the task text (kills ResNet and Cognitive Psychology 1974); working papers (NBER/SSRN) quarantined into a 'contextual-only' bin, never primary evidence — this reproduces cellcog's Webb move. (f) WEIGHT: quality = journal standing + citation count + recency bonus (2024-2026, cellcog's refs are 41/104 from 2024+) + method-texture availability (does the abstract state N, design, identification?). (g) DEDUP: DOI-canonical; fuzzy-title for DOI-less. (h) CONSOLIDATE into evidence cards (next layer). STOPPING CONDITION = COVERAGE MATRIX, not a number: rows = restructuring dimensions the task names (employment/displacement, wages, skills/task content, polarization, autonomy/surveillance/algorithmic management, labor share, gender, Global South/informality, institutions/policy) x industries (manufacturing, healthcare, finance, retail, transport/logistics, professional services, education, platform/gig) x eras (computerization, robots/narrow AI, generative AI) x levels (platform/firm/regional/national/informal-sector). STOP when every reachable cell holds >=2 on-topic journal works OR a declared literature gap (gaps get narrated as findings — 'the peer-reviewed evidence on X is thin because...', which cellcog does and which no faithfulness gate can cut), AND total >=120 on-topic journal works with >=35% dated 2024+, AND one full round adds <2 new on-topic works per 20 queries. Target 120-150 works vs cellcog's ~100 — evidence volume is the lane where we out-score rather than match.
SYNTHESIS: THE CORRECTED-CORRECTED LINE. The brief's fix ('a mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source') is necessary but NOT sufficient — grounding proved it would delete cellcog's highest-value section (§5 opens: 'syntheses that no single paper in the peer-reviewed literature articulates'), and 71% of cellcog's sentences carry no citation at all. FOUR LEGAL SENTENCE CLASSES: (1) GROUNDED FACT — asserts what a cited card's span states; entailment as before. (2) COMPOSITE BRIDGE (bodhi's winning move) — finding from paper A + mechanism from paper B, BOTH cited; fix /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py:197-205 so the mechanism check runs against the declared mechanisms of ANY cited card in the corpus, not only the two premises being compared. (3) ADJUDICATION — cited finding A + cited finding B + an uncited, HEDGED resolution that names the resolving dimension drawn from declared card fields (level/horizon/method): 'The parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration: firm-level aggregates can show growth while exposed workers face losses.' Legal iff the named dimension differs between the premise cards — deterministically checkable. (4) LABELED ANALYTICAL SYNTHESIS (cellcog's insight engine, the class the brief's rule bans) — an un-sourced mechanism/claim is legal IFF all three hold: (i) HEDGED verb from an allowlist ('may', 'could', 'we propose as analytical synthesis', 'suggests'); (ii) EPISTEMICALLY TAGGED with the graded taxonomy ([Established Finding] / [Emerging Pattern] / [Conceptual Argument] / [Analytical Hypothesis / Our Synthesis]); (iii) accompanied by an EXPLICIT GAP DECLARATION verified against the gap index ('No peer-reviewed study has yet measured...' — and our corpus confirms none does). That is cellcog's fabrication-free mechanism verbatim: assert the original claim AND tell the judge it is original and untested. STILL DELETED, DETERMINISTICALLY, ALWAYS: any number not in a source span; any study/organisation/author that does not exist; any attribution to someone who did not say it; any UNHEDGED-UNTAGGED mechanism no card states; forecasts stated as fact (hedged scenario-discussion tagged [Analytical Hypothesis] is legal; lunon's '2030 verdict' style is not); universals. The entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause stops being a sentence-killer and becomes a ROUTER: NEUTRAL sentences route to contract classes 2-4 instead of deletion.
COMPOSER: EXTEND /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py TO THE MEASURED #1 ARCHITECTURE — cellcog's actual moves, not the brief's misreadings. (1) CITATION TEMPLATE — cellcog's real workhorse (73 instances), NOT the brief's 'Writing in the JEP in 2019' contortion: 'Author(s) (YYYY), in the *Journal Name*, [verb] that ...' — author name and journal name in prose, YEAR STAYS IN PARENS (160/177 of cellcog's year-parens are this narrative form and it wins; the brief's 'year MUST be prose' rule is refuted). Zero [n] in body; references section retained for provenance (cleaner deletes it; costs nothing). (2) SPINE = THREE TECHNOLOGY ERAS (computerization/polarization → robots/narrow AI → generative AI) with sectors NESTED inside each era — cellcog's actual skeleton. 4IR handled by critique-and-subordinate: adopt as periodization in §2, contest it with journal-published critiques, decline the frame uncritically; NO four-revolutions comparison spine (that is WhaleCloud's move, 0.5396). (3) STRUCTURE: ~10 H2 + ~30 H3 + 6-8 H4; median paragraph ~100w, max <280w; body 12,500-14,000 words (cellcog 13,486; any 8k cap fights #1 with half its ammunition). (4) STRUCTURED ABSTRACT Objective/Methods/Findings/Contributions with a THESIS stated up front — our analogue of cellcog's 'Heterogeneity, not displacement, is the central peer-reviewed finding', derived from our contradiction index; Contributions line enumerates the syntheses by count. (5) SCOPE/METHODS/SOURCE-SELECTION section that ARGUES compliance: narrates journal-only criteria, analyzes the constraint as a publication bias with a stated compensation strategy, pre-empts the working-paper objection — plus point-of-use re-enactment whenever a contextual-only card appears ('Because X remains a working paper, we reference it contextually rather than as primary evidence'). (6) THE INSIGHT ENGINE: a dedicated 'Contested Debates and Novel Insights' H2 with 6-8 NAMED, BRANDED syntheses ('the informality buffer', 'the gender exposure reversal' style), each opened with first-person ownership, epistemically tagged via the graded taxonomy, hedged, and gap-declared — a numbered inventory of insights handed to the INSIGHT judge (weight 0.32). Include the legend paragraph: 'The convention is not cosmetic...'. (7) ONE MASTER KEY threaded through everything — level-of-analysis/institutional mediation at five named levels — so the document has a thesis, not coverage. (8) MONOGRAPH WIRING: ~30 internal cross-references ('a tension taken up in Section 5.3') — zero board systems except cellcog do this; cheap to generate in a second pass. (9) EPIGRAMMATIC PARAGRAPH CLOSES: unit of composition = evidence → evidence → one-line citation-free abstraction. (10) OPENING/CLOSING RING: open by staking a concrete evidentiary tension as 'the organizing puzzle', close by resolving it verbatim; final sentence refuses to forecast and positions the review above both sides of the public debate. NO meta-commentary opening ('This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence...' is deleted from every template). (11) SECTOR TABLE: 8 sectors x dominant technology generation x primary finding x anchor studies — the instruction-coverage matrix rendered as content; thin cells narrated as gap-findings. (12) METHODS TEXTURE mandatory on cited empirical claims: N, design, identification, in prose from card fields.
EXPECTED: Honest sum from 0.4382, per-dimension, weighted (insight .32/compreh .29/instr .25/readability .14): READABILITY 0.3774 → ~0.520 (the board's observed ceiling — every non-POLARIS system sits 0.51-0.52; the fix is fully mechanical: 100w paragraphs, H3/H4, no walls) = +0.020 weighted. INSIGHT 0.4238 → ~0.555 (the four-class synthesis lane + the dedicated insight section + adjudication density + thesis; targets cellcog's own mechanism at its own dimension, cellcog=0.5708) = +0.042 weighted. COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 → ~0.555 (120-150 on-topic journal works vs cellcog ~100, 13k body words, methods texture on every cited claim, sector matrix, recency through 2026 — the rubric's 'Data and Factual Support' and 'Depth and Representativeness' lanes, our genuine out-score lane) = +0.029 weighted. INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.4409 → ~0.552 (journal-only compliance argued in prose and at point of use, working-paper quarantine, sectors covered visibly, 4IR engaged-and-critiqued; cellcog=0.5530) = +0.028 weighted. TOTAL ≈ 0.4382 + 0.119 ≈ 0.557. VERDICT: this LANDS ON 0.5578, it does not safely BEAT it — the central estimate beats bodhi's measured 0.5441 by ~4x the resolvable effect, but beating 0.5578 by a resolvable margin (mean >= 0.567) depends on the wheel converting the comprehensiveness-and-verification edge (more sources, newer sources, visible methods texture cellcog cannot exceed without our corpus) into +0.01 above the first-pass estimate. That is what iterations 3+ exist for, and the honest statement is: first full pass ~0.545-0.557; beat-with-margin requires 2-4 wheel iterations and is plausible, not guaranteed.
CEILING: Three honesty items. (1) THE TARGET ITSELF IS SOFT: 0.5578 is cellcog's corpus-wide 100-task average; cellcog published no task-72 score. Regression across the 9 dual-scored systems puts its task-72 at ~0.556 with residual SD 0.0094 — the true target is ~0.556±0.019, possibly below bodhi's measured 0.5441, possibly ~0.575. We therefore run two thresholds: beat-best-measured (0.5441+margin, decidable) and beat-estimate (0.5578+margin, the stretch). (2) READABILITY IS CAPPED: nobody on the board exceeds ~0.52; the judge compresses this dim; budget no gains above it. (3) WHAT COULD KEEP US UNDER 0.5578: (a) the insight dimension may pay cellcog's 8 syntheses partly for genuine domain-expert quality, not just the labeled-hedged-gap FORM — if our class-4 sentences are formulaic, the judge's prose will say so and the ceiling is ~0.55; (b) retrieval may saturate below ~120 on-topic works if OA full-text coverage of 2024-2026 journals is poor — then comprehensiveness caps near parity with cellcog instead of above it; (c) cross-dimension bleed works both ways — a 13.5k-word report with any residual wall-of-text regressions taxes everything. If after the kill rule fires the plateau is 0.545-0.555, the honest report is: we beat every measured task-72 score on the board, and whether we beat cellcog is unmeasurable at judge noise — exactly the sentence the operator should get, not a proxy.

LENS:
FABRICATION: with the corrected line (mechanism allowed if ANY cited source states it), trace a concrete path by which a FALSE claim reaches the page. Fabricated facts/numbers/attributions must remain impossible.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""THE SYNTHESIS CONTRACT — the "deep thinking" half, without selling the faithfulness moat.

THE PROBLEM, PRECISELY
----------------------
POLARIS's entailment gate (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that introduces
"a fact, entity, MECHANISM ... NOT present in the SPAN". A cross-source inference IS, by definition,
a mechanism present in neither span. So our own gate deletes exactly the sentence type that earns
INSIGHT — 0.32 weight, the heaviest dimension, and our worst score (0.4238 vs bodhi's 0.5457).

We are not bad at analysis. We built a machine that cannot do it.

THE INSIGHT THAT MAKES THIS SOLVABLE
------------------------------------
Look at what the winning systems' synthesis prose actually asserts. cellcog, verbatim:

    "The three frameworks are complementary rather than competitive. SBTC remains the clearest model
     for relative skill demand. The task-based framework subsumes the phenomena both describe within
     a more general model of endogenous task allocation."

NO new fact. NO number. NO new entity. It RANKS and RELATES claims already on the page.
That is ADJUDICATION, and it is fully compatible with a no-fabrication guarantee.

So we do not relax the gate. We add a SECOND lane with a DIFFERENT PROOF OBLIGATION:

    EVIDENCE sentence  -> must be span-grounded in ONE source (the existing gate, UNCHANGED)
    SYNTHESIS sentence -> must be a TYPED RELATION over premises that are THEMSELVES already admitted

A synthesis sentence may not introduce a fact; it may only assert a relationship between facts that
are already proven. Its proof obligation is structural, not evidential.

THE HARD RULE THAT KEEPS US HONEST
----------------------------------
    An explanatory MECHANISM may appear ONLY if a premise span states that mechanism.
    Otherwise POLARIS may identify a contrast but MAY NOT explain its cause.

So this is legal:
    "Firm-level expansion and worker-level displacement are not contradictory: the two estimates
     observe different units of analysis."          [CONTRAST_LEVEL over two admitted premises]
And this is NOT, unless 'adoption lag' is stated in a premise:
    "The difference probably reflects slower regional adoption."   [imports an unproven mechanism]

Run the adversarial suite:  python scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import re
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path

# ---------------------------------------------------------------- the typed operations

OPERATIONS = {
    'CONVERGES':            'two or more premises agree on direction/finding',
    'CONTRASTS_DIRECTION':  'premises disagree on the direction of an effect',
    'CONTRASTS_LEVEL':      'premises differ because they observe different units (task/worker/firm/region/economy)',
    'CONTRASTS_HORIZON':    'premises differ because they observe different time horizons',
    'CONTRASTS_METHOD':     'premises differ because they use different identification strategies',
    'BOUNDARY_CONDITION':   'a premise holds only within a stated scope',
    'RANK_EVIDENCE':        'one premise rests on a stronger design than another (declared fields only)',
    'ESTABLISHES':          'the admitted evidence supports a claim AT A STATED LEVEL',
    'DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH':   'the admitted evidence does NOT support a claim at a stated level',
    'REMAINS_UNRESOLVED':   'the admitted evidence cannot distinguish between the competing accounts',
    'COVERAGE_GAP':         'a cell of the evidence matrix is empty — a real, derivable research gap',
}

# The rubric rewards ADJUDICATION, not hedging. This is the verdict vocabulary.
VERDICT_VOCAB = (
    'establishes', 'does not establish', 'supports', 'is limited to', 'cannot distinguish',
    'remains unresolved', 'are not contradictory', 'is consistent with', 'rests on',
    'observe different', 'holds only', 'no evidence', 'the evidence cannot', 'differ because',
    'complementary rather than', 'subsumes', 'rather than',
)

# A synthesis sentence may NEVER assert a causal mechanism of its own.
CAUSAL_IMPORT = re.compile(
    r'\b(because of|due to|caused by|driven by|reflects|reflecting|owing to|as a result of|'

r'\b(because of|due to|caused by|driven by|reflects|reflecting|owing to|as a result of|'
    r'attributable to|explains why|the reason is|stems from|arises from|leads to|results in)\b', re.I)

# Forecasts / predictions are always fabrication in a literature review.
FORECAST = re.compile(r'\b(will|shall|is going to|by 20[3-9]\d|in the coming|future will|predict|forecast)\b', re.I)

# Universal quantifiers = overclaim.
UNIVERSAL = re.compile(r'\b(all|every|always|never|none|no one|invariably|universally|certainly|proves)\b', re.I)

NUMERIC = re.compile(r'\d')
SPELLED_QTY = re.compile(
    r'\b(doubl|tripl|quadrupl|halv|tenfold|twofold|threefold|a third|a quarter|a half|two[- ]thirds|'
    r'percent|percentage|majority|most of|vast majority)\w*\b', re.I)
CAP_TOKEN = re.compile(r"\b([A-Z][A-Za-z&.\-']{1,})\b")

# Discourse vocabulary that may be capitalised without being a new entity.
SAFE_CAPS = {
    'The', 'This', 'These', 'Those', 'Taken', 'Together', 'Both', 'Neither', 'Either', 'While',
    'Whereas', 'Although', 'Because', 'When', 'Where', 'If', 'In', 'At', 'By', 'For', 'A', 'An',
    'It', 'They', 'Their', 'Its', 'However', 'Yet', 'Still', 'Across', 'Within', 'One', 'Two',
    'Three', 'Four', 'Five', 'Six', 'Seven', 'Eight', 'Nine', 'Ten', 'AI', 'Artificial',
    'Intelligence', 'Fourth', 'Industrial', 'Revolution', 'Evidence', 'Studies', 'Research',
    'Together', 'Read', 'Seen', 'Firm', 'Worker', 'Task', 'Aggregate', 'Nor', 'That', 'What',
    # ANALYTIC META-VOCABULARY: words that describe the EVIDENCE, not entities in the world.
    # These are the declared-field names ('level', 'horizon', 'method') and the language of
    # adjudication. Admitting them cannot fabricate anything — a fabricated NAME would still be
    # caught, because it would not appear in any premise.
    'level', 'Level', 'unit', 'Unit', 'analysis', 'Analysis', 'scope', 'Scope', 'design', 'Design',
    'horizon', 'Horizon', 'method', 'Method', 'estimate', 'Estimate', 'estimates', 'Estimates',
    'outcome', 'Outcome', 'outcomes', 'Outcomes', 'finding', 'Finding', 'findings', 'Findings',
    'economy', 'Economy', 'result', 'Result', 'results', 'Results', 'literature', 'Literature',
}


@dataclass
class Premise:
    """An ALREADY-ADMITTED evidence sentence — it passed the existing span-grounding gate."""
    id: str
    text: str
    source: str                      # attribution string, e.g. "Autor et al. (2003), QJE"
    level: str = ''                  # task | worker | firm | occupation | region | economy
    horizon: str = ''                # short-run | long-run
    method: str = ''                 # experiment | quasi-experimental | observational | survey
    mechanisms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # mechanisms STATED IN THE SPAN


@dataclass
class Synthesis:
    operation: str
    premise_ids: list[str]
    text: str


def _stem(w: str) -> str:
    """Crude stemmer. Needed because 'task level' must anchor to a premise that says 'tasks',
    and 'effect' to one that says 'effects'. Anchoring is a RELEVANCE check, not a safety check —
    loosening it cannot admit a fabrication (the entity/number/mechanism gates do that work)."""
    for suf in ('ies', 'es', 's'):
        if w.endswith(suf) and len(w) - len(suf) >= 4:
            return w[: -len(suf)]
    return w


def _content_lemmas(s: str) -> set[str]:
    return {_stem(w) for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', s.lower())}


def validate(syn: Synthesis, premises: dict[str, Premise]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Deterministic. No LLM. Returns (ok, reason_if_rejected)."""
    s = syn.text.strip()
    if not s:
        return False, 'empty'

    # 1. the operation must be one we defined
    if syn.operation not in OPERATIONS:
        return False, f'unknown_operation:{syn.operation}'

    # 2. premises must exist and be ADMITTED evidence
    prem = [premises[p] for p in syn.premise_ids if p in premises]
    if len(prem) != len(syn.premise_ids):
        missing = [p for p in syn.premise_ids if p not in premises]
        return False, f'premise_not_admitted:{missing}'

    # 3. relational operations need >= 2 premises from >= 2 DISTINCT sources
    relational = syn.operation not in ('COVERAGE_GAP',)
    if relational:
        if len(prem) < 2:
            return False, 'relational_operation_needs_2+_premises'
        if len({p.source for p in prem}) < 2:
            return False, 'premises_share_a_single_source'

    # 4. NO NEW NUMBER. Ever. (A number belongs to an EVIDENCE sentence, not a synthesis.)
    if NUMERIC.search(s):
        return False, 'synthesis_carries_a_digit'
    if SPELLED_QTY.search(s):
        return False, f'synthesis_carries_a_spelled_quantity:{SPELLED_QTY.search(s).group(0)}'

    # 5. NO NEW ENTITY. Every capitalised token must already appear in a premise or be discourse.
    #    Hyphenated compounds are checked COMPONENT-WISE ("Firm-level" -> "Firm", "level"), because a
    #    compound built from known words introduces no new entity. Each component still has to clear
    #    the same bar, so this cannot let a fabricated name through.
    blob = ' '.join(p.text + ' ' + p.source for p in prem)
    blob_lower = blob.lower()
    prem_caps = set(CAP_TOKEN.findall(blob))
    prem_stems = _content_lemmas(blob)
    for tok in CAP_TOKEN.findall(s):
        for part in re.split(r'[-–]', tok):
            if not part:
                continue
            if part in SAFE_CAPS or part in prem_caps:
                continue
            pl = part.lower()
            # a capitalised form of a word the premises already use (incl. plural/singular)
            if pl in blob_lower or _stem(pl) in prem_stems:
                continue
            return False, f'new_entity:{part}'

    # 6. NO IMPORTED MECHANISM. A causal claim is legal ONLY if a premise span states that mechanism.
    m = CAUSAL_IMPORT.search(s)
    if m:
        stated = {mm.lower() for p in prem for mm in p.mechanisms}
        if not stated:
            return False, f'causal_language_with_no_stated_mechanism:"{m.group(0)}"'
        # the mechanism named must actually be one the premises state
        if not any(mech in s.lower() for mech in stated):
            return False, f'causal_language_names_a_mechanism_no_premise_states:"{m.group(0)}"'

    # 7. NO FORECAST, NO UNIVERSAL
    if FORECAST.search(s):
        return False, f'forecast_or_prediction:"{FORECAST.search(s).group(0)}"'
    if UNIVERSAL.search(s):
        return False, f'universal_overclaim:"{UNIVERSAL.search(s).group(0)}"'

    # 8. must ADJUDICATE — the rubric pays for verdicts, not vibes
    if not any(v in s.lower() for v in VERDICT_VOCAB):
        return False, 'no_verdict_vocabulary (this is a vibe, not an adjudication)'

    # 9. operation-specific structural obligations, checked against DECLARED fields only
    if syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL':
        if len({p.level for p in prem if p.level}) < 2:
            return False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL but the premises do not declare 2 distinct levels'
    if syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_HORIZON':
        if len({p.horizon for p in prem if p.horizon}) < 2:
            return False, 'CONTRASTS_HORIZON but the premises do not declare 2 distinct horizons'
    if syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_METHOD' or syn.operation == 'RANK_EVIDENCE':
        if len({p.method for p in prem if p.method}) < 2:
            return False, f'{syn.operation} but the premises do not declare 2 distinct methods'

    # 10. must be anchored in the premises it claims to relate
    if len(_content_lemmas(s) & _content_lemmas(blob)) < 2:
        return False, 'not_anchored_in_its_premises'

    return True, ''


# ---------------------------------------------------------------- adversarial suite

P = {
    'p1': Premise('p1', 'Generative AI raised the productivity of professional writing tasks by 40 percent.',
                  'Noy and Zhang (2023), Science', level='task', horizon='short-run', method='experiment'),
    'p2': Premise('p2', 'Aggregate employment effects in AI-exposed occupations were too small to detect over the period studied.',
                  'Acemoglu et al. (2022), Journal of Labor Economics', level='economy', horizon='long-run',

    'p3': Premise('p3', 'Firms adopting AI expanded employment relative to non-adopting competitors.',

                  method='observational'),
    'p4': Premise('p4', 'Routine task-intensive middle-wage occupations declined as computerisation spread, '
                        'a process the authors attribute to task displacement.',
                  'Autor et al. (2003), Quarterly Journal of Economics', level='occupation', horizon='long-run',
                  method='observational', mechanisms=['task displacement']),
}

     'The difference probably reflects slower regional adoption of the technology.',
     'imports a mechanism (adoption lag) no premise states'),
    (False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p1', 'p2'],
     'Task-level gains of 40 percent do not establish economy-wide employment growth.',
     'carries a digit — that belongs to an EVIDENCE sentence'),
    (False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p1', 'p2'],

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""CLEANER-SURVIVAL TEST — which citation format survives RACE's LLM cleaner?

THE STAKES: RACE runs an LLM "ArticleCleaner" over every report BEFORE the judge sees it, instructed to
"remove all citation links, citation marks (such as [1], [2] ...), reference lists, footnotes".
VERIFIED on our own artifact: 345 [n] markers -> 0, and our entire 105-entry bibliography -> deleted.

So the judge currently sees POLARIS as a report with NO SOURCES AT ALL, on a task whose instruction is
"Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles."

The #1 system (cellcog) instead writes "Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019), in the Journal of Economic
Perspectives, show..." — ordinary prose. We BELIEVE the cleaner cannot remove that. But GPT-5.6 Sol
made the correct objection: **the cleaner is an LLM, not a regex.** Its prompt says "or other complex
citation formats". Whether author-year attribution survives is an EMPIRICAL question, and the entire
attribution lever rests on the answer.

This runs the PRODUCTION cleaner over the same 12 factual sentences rendered in 5 competing formats,
N times each, and reports what survives. No guessing.

Usage:
  set -a && . ./.env && set +a
  python scripts/cleaner_survival_test.py --runs 3
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
DRB = ROOT / 'third_party' / 'deep_research_bench'
sys.path.insert(0, str(DRB))

I ran the actual code. Here are the traces.

## The executable proof (the plan's own named edit breaks the repo's own fabrication test)

`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py` gate #6 is sound for exactly one reason: the mechanism pool is **the premises of the sentence being checked**. The plan's directive — *"fix synthesis_contract.py:197-205 so the mechanism check runs against the declared mechanisms of ANY cited card in the corpus, not only the two premises being compared"* — deletes that scoping. I emulated it:

```
sentence: "...a divergence that reflects adoption lag across firms."   [cites p1, p2 only]
TODAY  (premise-scoped pool)      -> (False, 'causal_language_with_no_stated_mechanism:"reflects"')
PLAN   (corpus-wide pool)         -> (True, '')
```

That sentence is verbatim the repo's own ATTACK #1 (`'imports a mechanism (adoption lag) no premise states'`). The plan converts a suite the codebase ships as a fabrication regression test into a pass. And it does so **for no gain**: bodhi's composite bridge cites paper B, so B *is* a premise, and the current premise-scoped code already admits it. The edit overshoots its own stated purpose by exactly the unsafe delta — it admits mechanisms from cards the sentence does **not** cite. The output is a causal claim rendered in prose with narrative attributions to A and B, neither of whom said it: **a fabricated attribution, produced deterministically.** Worse, the check is a substring test (`any(mech in s.lower() for mech in stated)`); with 120-150 cards the pool becomes the union of every mechanism in the field ("automation", "adoption", "displacement", "monitoring", "deskilling"), so gate #6 degenerates into "does the sentence contain a word the field uses." It is not weakened; it is disabled.

## Trace 2 — the NEUTRAL router turns the hallucination detector into a formatter

`entailment_judge.py:588` defines NEUTRAL as *"the SENTENCE introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN."* That verdict **is** the hallucination signal. The plan: *"The entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause stops being a sentence-killer and becomes a ROUTER: NEUTRAL sentences route to contract classes 2-4 instead of deletion."*

Concrete path: composer emits *"Algorithmic management in warehouse logistics may raise injury rates by compressing task cycle times below recovery thresholds,"* intending to cite card A (a surveillance study that says nothing about injury rates). Judge → NEUTRAL. Today: deleted. Under the plan: routed to class 4, which requires only (i) a hedge verb, (ii) `[Analytical Hypothesis / Our Synthesis]`, (iii) a gap declaration. All three are **costume, not proof**. Nothing in class 4 requires the claim's content to be derivable from any admitted premise — class 4 has no premise list, therefore gate #5 (new-entity, checked against the premise blob) and gate #4 (no new number) have nothing to check against and cannot run. The system's only detector of unsupported content now *triggers publication* instead of deletion.

Note the specification gap against the model being copied: cellcog's actual class-4 sentences are *conditional extrapolations over cited findings* ("If generative AI disproportionately exposes female-dominated work **and** women adopt it less, the gender wage gap could…" — built on Dorta-González 2024, cited). The plan's rule licenses **any** un-sourced mechanism wearing three tags. It copies cellcog's costume, not cellcog's constraint.

## Trace 3 — gap declarations are false claims *manufactured on a schedule*

Class-4 condition (iii) *mandates* "No peer-reviewed study has yet measured X," verified "against the gap index" — i.e., against a 120-150-item snowball whose stopping rule is local saturation (`<2 new works per 20 queries`), not literature coverage (the AI-and-labour literature is 10⁴+ papers). "No peer-reviewed study has measured X" is a **universal negative existential over the world's literature asserted from a convenience sample**. It is false whenever such a study sits outside the corpus — and the error is *systematically correlated*: you make the gap claim precisely in the thin cells where your retrieval is weakest. This is a fabricated fact of the most damaging kind (a claim about the state of a field), mandated 6-8+ times per report, and **structurally uncheckable** — no span gate can verify the absence of spans. The plan names this as a feature: *"gaps get narrated as findings … which no faithfulness gate can cut."* That is not a moat; it is a written admission that the plan has an unguarded channel. It also violates the plan's own STILL-DELETED list ("universals") and would require weakening the repo's `UNIVERSAL` regex to ship.

## Trace 4 — mandatory methods texture over optional source fields is a hallucination pump

The no-fabrication floor is "any number not in a source span is deleted." The plan's span is an **evidence card** (retrieval step (h): "CONSOLIDATE into evidence cards"), and the composer mandates *"METHODS TEXTURE mandatory on cited empirical claims: N, design, identification, in prose from card fields."* The plan never states cards must be verbatim-extractive with offsets. So: LLM writes card → card becomes the trusted span → composer cites card → number gate passes. A hallucinated N is laundered into a "grounded fact." And Crossref abstracts routinely omit N and identification (the plan itself permits metadata-only cards). A **required output field with an absent input** is the canonical condition under which a model invents. The failure mode is a fabricated sample size welded to a real author, real journal, real DOI — the worst possible artifact for a system whose pitch is verification.

## Trace 5 — narrative citations upgrade every binding error into a defamation-shaped fabrication

The plan deletes all [n] markers and moves attribution into prose ("Author(s) (YYYY), in the *Journal Name*, show that…"). Under [n], an off-by-one card binding is a wrong number. In narrative form it is a **false factual claim about a named person and a named journal**. The entailment judge compares SENTENCE to SPAN; the author name lives in the sentence, not the span, so nothing checks that the prose name/venue match the card. And the venue string comes from Crossref `container-title`, which returns "SSRN Electronic Journal" / "NBER Working Paper Series" / preprint mirrors for a nontrivial share of records — so both the working-paper quarantine *and* the in-prose journal name ride on an unreliable field.

## Trace 6 — class-3 certifies causation with a metadata diff

"Legal iff the named dimension differs between the premise cards — deterministically checkable." Two cards differing on `level` proves they differ on `level`; it does **not** prove the level difference *explains* their divergent findings (which may diverge by period, country, or coding error). The contract stamps an uncited causal explanation as verified. And the plan's own exemplar — "The parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration: firm-level aggregates can show growth while exposed workers face losses" — contains no hedge, violating the plan's own "uncited, HEDGED resolution" rule in the sentence it chose to showcase.

## Verdict

The lens question was: *trace a concrete path by which a FALSE claim reaches the page.* There are six, one of them executable against the repo's own test suite, one of them (gap declarations) **mandatory on every insight sentence**, and one of them (the NEUTRAL router) sitting on the mainline of every paragraph. The plan's stated invariant — "fabricated facts/numbers/attributions must remain impossible" — is asserted in prose and contradicted by the three specific code changes the plan names. It is not that the plan takes a measured risk; it is that the plan believes the floor still holds while removing the scoping that holds it.

Key files: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py` (gates 4-7, ATTACKS suite), `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:588`, `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cleaner_survival_test.py`.

input.fix:
Keep the strategy (insight engine, monograph architecture, journal-first retrieval) but restore scoping to the three places the plan breaks it:

1. DO NOT widen the mechanism pool to the corpus. Widen it to the UNION OF THE SENTENCE'S OWN CITED CARDS. That is all bodhi's composite bridge needs (if the mechanism comes from paper B and B is cited, B is a premise). Add a compatibility guard: a mechanism may cross from card B to a finding from card A only if B and A share at least one declared dimension (era / sector / level) — blocks transplanting a 1980s-computerisation mechanism onto Kenyan gig work. Re-run scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test and require ATTACK #1 to still REJECT; make that a CI gate.

2. CLASS 4 MUST CARRY PREMISES. Change the rule from "un-sourced mechanism legal if hedged+tagged+gap-declared" to "an ANALYTICAL SYNTHESIS is legal iff it is a CONDITIONAL over >=2 admitted premises (cellcog's actual form: 'If [premise A] and [premise B], then X could...'), AND hedged, AND tagged." Then it still passes validate() with premise_ids, so gates #4 (no new number) and #5 (no new entity) still run. This is what cellcog actually does and it costs nothing on the insight dimension.

3. NEUTRAL IS NOT A ROUTER. A NEUTRAL verdict may only trigger a REWRITE-AND-RE-JUDGE loop (rewrite the sentence to be entailed by its span, or re-route to a class that supplies premises and re-validate). A sentence that cannot pass any class with premises is DELETED, as today. Never allow "add a hedge + a tag" to convert a NEUTRAL into a publication.

4. GAP CLAIMS MUST BE CORPUS-SCOPED IN PROSE, ALWAYS. Ban the bare "No peer-reviewed study has measured X." Mandate the scoped form: "Within the 137 journal articles retrieved for this review, none measures X" or cellcog's hedge "questions the peer-reviewed literature has not yet answered." This preserves the entire insight/instruction payoff (the judge still sees gap-narration and epistemic honesty) while making the claim TRUE by construction. Add a deterministic regex gate rejecting unscoped negative existentials.

5. EVIDENCE CARDS MUST BE EXTRACTIVE. Every card field usable as a span (N, design, identification, finding, mechanism) must be a verbatim quote with a character offset into the fetched abstract/full text, or be NULL. Methods texture is emitted ONLY from non-null fields; missing texture is narrated as missing ("the abstract does not report the sample size"), never filled. No LLM-written field may ever serve as the ground-truth span for the number gate.

6. ATTRIBUTION BINDING CHECK. Add a deterministic gate: every author surname and journal name appearing in prose must string-match the `authors`/`container-title` of a card the sentence is entailed against. Reject on mismatch. Cross-check container-title against a journal allowlist (ISSN) so SSRN/NBER records can never be rendered in prose as journals.

7. CLASS 3 MUST HEDGE AND MUST NOT CLAIM CAUSATION. Allowed: "these estimates observe different units of analysis" (a fact about the cards). Banned: "the divergence IS EXPLAINED BY the level difference" (a causal claim a metadata diff cannot support). Fix the plan's own exemplar accordingly.
